qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via unvalidated redirect URLs in HAR replay
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes Playwright HAR replay redirecting to attacker-controlled URLs from HAR entries; the advisory describes @playwright/mcp missing Origin header validation on incoming MCP server connections enabling DNS rebinding and unauthorized tool invocation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded WebSocket message size enables memory exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an unbounded WebSocket payload size and memory exhaustion condition in Playwright server code; the advisory root cause is absent Origin validation in @playwright/mcp that permits DNS rebinding requests to MCP tool endpoints.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described @playwright/mcp missing Origin header validation or an equivalent DNS-rebinding path to MCP tool invocation.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6fg3-hvw7-2fwq targets @playwright/mcp versions before 0.0.40 and states that incoming connections fail to validate the Origin header, enabling DNS rebinding from a victim browser to invoke MCP tool endpoints.
har-ssrf/vulnerability_report.json targets HAR replay redirect handling in harBackend.ts, harRouter.ts, and frame navigation, not MCP server Origin validation.
ws-dos-message-size/vulnerability_report.json targets missing WebSocket maxPayload enforcement and JSON parsing of oversized messages, not DNS rebinding or unauthorized MCP tool access.
